name.text: Finding volume of a frustum given dimensions.

questiontext.text:
A frustum has a bottom radius of {#R#} cm, a top radius of {#r#} cm, and a height of {#h#} cm.

[[jsxgraph width="400px" height="400px"]] var board = JXG.JSXGraph.initBoard(divid, { /* Bounding box set to comfortably fit the max scaled values (Rdraw max=5, hdraw max=10) */ boundingbox: [-8, 12, 8, -4], axis: false, showCopyright: false }); /* 1. Pull the random variables directly from STACK */ var R = {#R#}; var r = {#r#}; var h = {#h#}; var Rdraw = {#Rdraw#}; var rdraw = {#rdraw#}; var hdraw = {#hdraw#}; /* 2. Define the structural points */ var C0 = board.create('point', [0, 0], {visible: false}); // Bottom center var C1 = board.create('point', [0, hdraw], {visible: false}); // Top center var RB = board.create('point', [Rdraw, 0], {visible: false}); // Right bottom var RT = board.create('point', [rdraw, hdraw], {visible: false});// Right top var LB = board.create('point', [-Rdraw, 0], {visible: false}); // Left bottom var LT = board.create('point', [-rdraw, hdraw], {visible: false});// Left top /* 3. Draw the solid sides of the bucket */ board.create('segment', [LB, LT], {strokeWidth: 2, strokeColor: '#333333'}); board.create('segment', [RB, RT], {strokeWidth: 2, strokeColor: '#333333'}); /* 4. Draw the 3D ellipses for top and bottom */ // Top face (solid) board.create('curve', [ function(t){ return rdraw * Math.cos(t); }, function(t){ return hdraw + (rdraw * 0.25) * Math.sin(t); }, 0, 2*Math.PI ], {strokeWidth: 2, strokeColor: '#333333', fillColor: '#e0f7fa', fillOpacity: 0.8}); // Bottom face (front edge solid, back edge dashed) board.create('curve', [ function(t){ return Rdraw * Math.cos(t); }, function(t){ return (Rdraw * 0.25) * Math.sin(t); }, Math.PI, 2*Math.PI ], {strokeWidth: 2, strokeColor: '#333333'}); board.create('curve', [ function(t){ return Rdraw * Math.cos(t); }, function(t){ return (Rdraw * 0.25) * Math.sin(t); }, 0, Math.PI ], {strokeWidth: 1, dash: 2, strokeColor: '#666666'}); /* 5. Draw dashed internal lines for Height and Radii */ board.create('segment', [C0, C1], {strokeWidth: 2, dash: 2, strokeColor: '#ff0000'}); // Height board.create('segment', [C1, RT], {strokeWidth: 2, dash: 2, strokeColor: '#0000ff'}); // Top Radius board.create('segment', [C0, RB], {strokeWidth: 2, dash: 2, strokeColor: '#0000ff'}); // Bottom Radius /* 6. Add dynamic text labels */ // Top radius label board.create('text', [rdraw/2 - 0.5, hdraw + 0.5, r + " cm"], {fontSize: 14}); // Bottom radius label board.create('text', [Rdraw/2 - 0.5, -0.8, R + " cm"], {fontSize: 14}); // Height label board.create('text', [-1.5, hdraw/2, h + " cm"], {fontSize: 14, strokeColor: '#ff0000'}); [[/jsxgraph]]

Calculate the volume of the container in litres.

(Recall \(1\text{ litre}=1000\text{ cm}^3\))
Write \(\pi\) as %pi and \(\times\) as *

Volume = [[input:ans1]] litres [[validation:ans1]] [[feedback:prt1]]

generalfeedback.text:
Worked solution

To find the volume of a frustum we use \[V=\frac{1}{3}\pi h(R^2+Rr+r^2) \]

Where: \( R\) = Bottom (larger) radius
\(r\) = Top (smaller) radius
\(h\) = Vertical height

Substitute the given values of \(R, r\) and \(h\) into the formula
\(R^2 = {@R@}^2 = {@R^2@}\)
\(r^2 = {@r@}^2 = {@r^2@} \)
\(R \times r = {@R@} \times {@r@} = {@R*r@}\)

Solving for \(R^2 + Rr + r^2\):
\({@R^2@} + {@R@} \times {@r@} + {@r^2@} = {@bracket_value@}\)

Next multiply \({@bracket_value@}\) by the height
\({@bracket_value@} \times {@h@} = {@height_bracket_value@}\)

Now we have :
\( V= \frac{1}{3}\pi \times {@height_bracket_value@}\)

\(= {@V@}\)

Converting cm\(^3\) to litres we have:

\(= \frac{{@V@} \,\,\text{cm}^3} {1000\,\, \text{cm}^3} \times 1\,\, \text{litre} \)

\(= {@ta@}\)

Hence \(V= {@ta@}\) litres

prtcorrect.text: Correct answer, well done.

prtpartiallycorrect.text: Your answer is partially correct.

prtincorrect.text: Incorrect answer.

node.description: Checks for the correct volume of a frustum

truefeedback.text: It looks like you calculated the volume in cm\(^3\) but forgot to convert it to litres. Please look at the worked solution below.
